item.title: The Black Hills Powwow … with a city symbol included
item.description: That flag is that of General Custer of the 7th Cavalry, wiped out at the Battle of Little Big Horn.
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.title: Suit Up! It's time to go to Antarctica
item.description:
In order to protect us from the elements, we are issued Extreme Cold Weather gear (ECW). We are allowed up to 85 lbs of checked luggage, but we have to make room within that weight for our ECW (estimated at about 15 lbs)
This board shows all the different ECW clothing that could be issued. Not everyone gets everything. A lot of the clothing assignment is job-related
We have to inventory our ECW bags to ensure that everything fits and that all the zippers and fasteners work. You wouldn't want to be out in the elements and not be able to close your jacket. First-year participants are required to take all issued gear. Whereas returning participants may opt out of certain items
ECW bags for a full flight
We're allowed up to 85 lbs of luggage to take South with us. We are instructed to make room within that 85 lbs to take our ECW with us (about 15 lbs)
Suited up and ready to go
May the spirit of Robert Falcon Scott guide us safely on our expedition
Now that we have our gear, let's go to The Ice. It's about a 5-hour flight to McMurdo from Christchurch, NZ
Made it safely. Here's a bunch of us pouring out of the C-17 and on to the Ross Ice Shelf at Phoenix Airfield. Wheeled aircraft need at least six feet of ice thickness in order to land. From here it's about an hour drive into "town" to get to McMurdo
Bronze Antarctic Sled Dog tax. Don't worry, he's a good boy. Not some kinda Thing!
item.dc:creator: VosperOfAntarctica

item.title: Natural looking
item.description: I hope you smile or crack a mental smirk at some point today, from this or an unrelated thing - even if only a brief moment. Cheers!!

item.title: The future of medical education (now with sources)
item.description: https://he.kendallhunt.com/product/human-anatomy-5 In case you want to know why darn near every science fiction story with AI in it has the thing turning evil, THIS is the sorts of forms that that evil takes BEFORE it starts launching nukes.

item.title: 10 years?
item.description:
That's crazy. It's been ten years. So much has happened. What a crazy decade.
We had a catnip party.
Chessie is digging it
Puddles is...uh... Gonna take some savings and get Chinese food for the holiday tomorrow! Maybe. Maybe we just eat Mac and cheese. Lol. I don't know if imgur is gonna last another decade, honestly, but if not, I'd like to be actual pen pals with some of you. Let me know. I need a hobby that requires some sort of socialization.
item.dc:creator: TinyOctopus

item.title: Yankee Pot Roast for a classic Nor’easter
item.description:
When a nor’easter parks itself off the Jersey coast, there’s no better time to stay in and let something slow-cook in the oven. This pot roast fits the mood precisely. It’s steady, familiar, and built for weather like this. The wind can do what it wants outside. Inside, the kitchen smells like rosemary, red wine, and garlic. It’s the kind of meal that makes the house feel sturdy again, the kind you can ladle out while the storm keeps moving through.
A little fresh parsley from the garden brings much needed color and a fresh note to balance all of the earthy flavors going on in this dish.
I don’t know how to make this slow motion, but I think it’s darn sexy.
Yankee Pot Roast Ingredients: Rosemary Salt: 1 tablespoon kosher salt 1 teaspoon freshly cracked black pepper 1 tablespoon fresh rosemary, finely chopped Meat Prep: 4-pound beef chuck roast 2 tablespoons neutral oil (canola or vegetable) Deglazing & Sauce Base: 1 large onion, diced 8 garlic cloves, lightly smashed 2 tablespoons tomato paste 3 tablespoons flour ﻿﻿2 tablespoons oil (if needed, after flour is added) ﻿﻿1 cup red wine ﻿﻿1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce ﻿﻿1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar ﻿﻿1 teaspoon soy sauce ﻿﻿½ teaspoon anchovy paste Broth Additions (Added in 3 Stages): 4 cups beef broth or stock, added in 3 parts Vegetables & Herbs (Added Later): 4 large carrots, cut into chunks 1/2 pound baby potatoes (or large potatoes cut into chunks) 2 stalks celery 8 sprigs fresh thyme 2 sprigs rosemary Instructions: 1. Prep & Sear the Meat 1. ﻿﻿﻿Preheat oven to 325°F (163°C). 2. ﻿﻿﻿Mix salt, pepper, and chopped rosemary to create the rosemary salt. 3. ﻿﻿﻿Pat the chuck roast dry with paper towels and season generously on all sides with the rosemary salt mixture. 4. ﻿﻿﻿Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a stainless steel pan over medium-high heat. 5. ﻿﻿﻿Sear the beef for 3-4 minutes per side until deeply browned. Remove and set aside. 6. Reduce heat to medium and add the diced onion. Sauté for 2-3 minutes, scraping up browned bits. 7. Add garlic cloves (I screwed up and minced it, but used it anyway) , cook for 1 more minute. Transfer mixture to enameled pot with lid, for the rest of the recipe. Put pot on medium heat. 8. ﻿﻿﻿Stir in 2 tablespoons tomato paste, cooking for 5 minutes until slightly darkened. 9. ﻿﻿﻿Reduce heat to medium-low, then add 3 tablespoons flour. Stir constantly for 1-2 minutes. 10. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Add 2 tablespoons oil (if needed) and cook for another 3 minutes, making sure the flour doesn't burn. 11. ﻿Pour in 1 cup red wine, scraping up any remaining bits from the bottom. Simmer for 5 minutes to reduce. 12. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Stir in 1 tablespoon Worcestershire sauce, 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar, soy sauce, and anchovy paste. 13. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Slowly add 4 cups of beef broth in 3 additions, a little over 1 cup at a time, bringing it to a simmer and letting it cook for 5 minutes between each addition. 14. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Return the seared chuck roast to the pot, ensuring it's mostly submerged. 15. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Cover with a cracked lid (slightly ajar) to allow for some evaporation. 16. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Place in a 325°F oven for 2 hours. 17. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Remove the pot from the oven, add the carrots, potatoes, celery, rosemary, and thyme sprigs, ensuring they are partially submerged in the broth. 18. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Cover again (lid still cracked) and return to the oven for another 90 minutes. 19. Remove from the oven and let the roast rest for 45 minutes in the covered pot. 20. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Skim any excess fat from the surface and remove the thyme sprigs. 21. ﻿﻿﻿﻿Serve sliced or shredded with the vegetables and rich broth, and some nice warm rolls or fresh bread.

item.title: My Hawkgirl Cosplay for NYCC
item.description: This cosplay was inspired by the amazing Jim Lee and his hawkgirl art (pictured below). The gauntlets, hand armor, belt buckle, and claws were 3D printed and finished by myself. I made the wings, all the leather straps, and the boots as well. The spandex suit was designed by @Braekor and the helmet and sword were made by my good friend @mickeymakes3d :)

item.title: neurospicy solidarity
item.description:
autism acceptance requires accepting weird people
autisic people accommodate non-autistic people all the time
ADHD are you sure you made the tea
ADHD Depot - symptoms
ADHD iceberg
ADHD makes you bad at capitalism
ADHD 38 symptoms
ADHD visualized (with sound)
autism awareness - changed to blue light bulbs
autism awareness month - look behind you
Autism Spectrum (it's a multi-symptom circle, not a line)
autistic authors writing neurotypical characters
autistic empathy behaviors
autistic kindergartner
autistic people in the past
Magic The Gathering - Chicken of Executive Function card
neurodivergent person in a dark room
"he doesn't look autistic" "do an autism for the lady"
everything has sound
executive dysfunction - do not take off your shoes
executive dysfunction - shame is not effective treatment
executive dysfunction - to do: take shower
executive dysfunction - too many steps to make coffee
if I show symptoms
ineffective mental health advice
it's all in your head - Ass Don't Have Dopamine
microwave the forgotten coffee
mild autism, spicy autism, flamin' hot autism
mind wanderer
mom of multiple ADHD kids, effective coping mechanisms
motivation - neurotypical vs neurodivergent
myth of the Changeling Child
navigate the world with autism - "draw a small clock"
neurodivergent self-awareness
neurodivergent love languages - long
neurodivergent love languages - short
out of kids' control
panic attack - ice cube in mouth
pathologised/pathologized allistic experience symptoms
people don't actually go from 0 to 60. you have failed to notice how long they've been at 59.
partially treated mental illness - "I won't perform my rock bottom for you for the sake of being believed."
This prison of flesh cannot hold me forever." - classroom poster of quiet, calm student
resilience to mental illness
routine - planned or unplanned divergence - "A cat may go in a box of its own accord, but it does not want to be put in a box."
solidarity and why it matters
disabled, or not?
taking calls right away - phone calls can be exhausting
talking about a passion, and having been stifled for it
almost everyone is traumatized - "We have never seen a totally sane human being." – Robert Anton Wilson
why do you need a label
item.dc:creator: trigonman3

item.title: Phone-clearing dump 335
item.description:
Oh look! It’s why I do these dumps
The background to this is too sad. The little girl that voiced Anne-Marie was killed. Burt Reynolds had a such hard time recording this last scene (Anne-Marie had already recorded her part), that it took over 60 takes
Go away…di*k
